message.content:
{
  "competency_groups": [
    {
      "code": "C1",
      "title": "Measurement and Quantitative Analysis",
      "description": "Competencies related to understanding matter, measurement uncertainties, laws of chemical combination, atomic theory, mole concept, stoichiometry, percentage composition, volumetric analysis, and quantitative estimation.",
      "competencies": [
        "Understand the nature of matter and its properties including uncertainty in measurement",
        "Apply Laws of Chemical Combination and Dalton's Atomic Theory to explain mass relationships",
        "Calculate atomic/molecular masses using mole concept and molar masses",
        "Perform stoichiometric calculations for percentage composition and reaction yields",
        "Execute volumetric analysis techniques (titrations) with standard solutions like oxalic acid and sodium hydroxide",
        "Determine concentration, strength of solutions, and perform quantitative estimations"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry / Solutions",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 1: Some Basic Concepts of Chemistry / Unit 1: Solutions"
    },
    {
      "code": "C2",
      "title": "Atomic Structure and Quantum Mechanics",
      "description": "Competencies covering the discovery of sub-atomic particles, evolution of atomic models from Bohr to quantum mechanical model, electronic configurations, and periodic classification.",
      "competencies": [
        "Trace the historical development of atomic models (Rutherford, Bohr) leading to the quantum mechanical model",
        "Explain the behavior of electrons in hydrogen atom using Bohr's theory",
        "Apply principles of Quantum Mechanics to describe electron distribution in atoms",
        "Determine electronic configurations and classify elements into s-, p-, d-, f- blocks based on periodic table position"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Structure of Atom / Classification of Elements...",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 2: Structure of Atom / Unit 3: Periodicity in Properties"
    },
    {
      "code": "C3",
      "title": "Chemical Bonding and Molecular Geometry",
      "description": "Competencies involving approaches to chemical bonding, types of bonds (ionic/covalent), bond parameters, VSEPR theory, hybridization, molecular orbital theory, and hydrogen bonding.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate between Kossel-Lewis approach and modern theories of chemical bonding",
        "Predict the geometry of molecules using Valence Shell Electron Pair Repulsion (VSEPR) theory",
        "Explain hybridization concepts and their role in molecular structure formation",
        "Apply Molecular Orbital Theory to explain bonding in homonuclear diatomic molecules"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Chemical Bonding...",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 4: Chemical Bonding and Molecular Structure"
    },
    {
      "code": "C4",
      "title": "Thermodynamics and Equilibrium Principles",
      "description": "Competencies focused on thermodynamic terms, enthalpy changes (calorimetry), spontaneity, Gibbs energy, equilibrium constants, Le Chatelier's principle, ionic equilibria, buffer solutions, and solubility.",
      "competencies": [
        "Calculate internal energy change ($\\Delta U$) and enthalpy change ($\\Delta H$) using calorimetry",
        "Determine the spontaneity of reactions based on Gibbs Energy changes and equilibrium conditions",
        "Apply Le Chatelier's principle to predict shifts in chemical equilibria due to concentration/temperature changes",
        "Analyze ionic equilibria including acid-base ionization, buffer solutions, and solubility products"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Thermodynamics / Equilibrium",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 5: Thermodynamics / Unit 6: Equilibrium"
    },
    {
      "code": "C5",
      "title": "Redox Reactions and Electrode Processes",
      "description": "Competencies related to classical ideas of oxidation/reduction, electron transfer concepts, oxidation numbers, redox reactions in electrode processes, electrochemical cells, batteries, and corrosion.",
      "competencies": [
        "Identify oxidizing and reducing agents using oxidation number rules"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Redox Reactions / Electrochemistry",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 7: Redox Reactions / Unit 2: Electrochemistry (Class XII)"
    },
    {
      "code": "C6",
      "title": "Organic Chemistry Fundamentals and Mechanisms",
      "description": "Competencies covering tetravalence of carbon, structural representations, nomenclature, isomerism, reaction mechanisms, purification techniques (distillation, crystallization), qualitative/quantitative analysis, and functional group identification.",
      "competencies": [
        "Represent organic compounds using various structural formulas and apply IUPAC nomenclature rules",
        "Identify different types of isomers in organic molecules"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Organic Chemistry: Some basic Principles...",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 8: Organic Chemistry - Basic Principles"
    },
    {
      "code": "C7",
      "title": "Hydrocarbons and Functional Group Chemistry",
      "description": "Competencies involving classification of hydrocarbons (alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, aromatics), preparation methods, physical/chemical properties, reactions of haloalkanes/haloarenes, alcohols, phenols, ethers, carbonyl compounds, and amines.",
      "competencies": [
        "Distinguish between saturated and unsaturated hydrocarbons based on structure and reactivity",
        "Predict products of chemical reactions for various functional groups (halides, alcohols, ketones, etc.)"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Hydrocarbons / Haloalkanes... Aldehydes...",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 9: Hydrocarbons / Unit 6-10: Organic Chemistry Topics"
    },
    {
      "code": "C8",
      "title": "d and f Block Elements and Coordination Compounds",
      "description": "Competencies covering position in periodic table, electronic configurations of transition elements, general properties (magnetic behavior, catalytic activity), lanthanoids/actinoids, Werner's theory, nomenclature, isomerism, bonding theories, and applications.",
      "competencies": [
        "Explain the trends in physical and chemical properties across d-block and f-block series"
      ],
      "unit_name": "d and f Block Elements / Coordination Compounds",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 4: d and f Block Elements (Class XII) / Unit 5: Coordination Compounds"
    },
    {
      "code": "C9",
      "title": "Surface Chemistry, Kinetics, and Biomolecules",
      "description": "Competencies related to adsorption/desorption phenomena, colloidal states (lyophilic/lyophobic), emulsions, reaction rates, factors affecting kinetics (temperature/concentration), collision theory, carbohydrates, proteins, enzymes, vitamins, nucleic acids, and hormones.",
      "competencies": [
        "Differentiate between lyophilic and lyophobic sols and explain the role of emulsifying agents"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Surface Chemistry / Chemical Kinetics / Biomolecules",
      "chapter_name": "Unit 10: Surface Chemistry (Class XII) / Unit 3: Kinetics / Unit 10: Biomolecules"
    },
    {
      "code": "C10",
      "title": "Laboratory Skills and Practical Applications",
      "description": "Competencies involving basic laboratory techniques, characterization of substances (melting/boiling points), purification methods (crystallization), pH determination, volumetric analysis, qualitative inorganic analysis (cation/anion detection), chromatography, and project-based scientific investigations.",
      "competencies": [
        "Perform standard laboratory operations including cutting glass tubes, boring corks, and setting up apparatus"
      ],
      "unit_name": "Basic Laboratory Techniques / Practical Syllabus",
      "chapter_name": "Practicals: Basic Lab Techniques & Experiments"
    }
  ]
}
